summary: Response-head pre-read diagnostics show ylong_http_client reads the full 4096-byte response body during response-head decoding in every measured request. This confirms full-body pre-read is real, but the same run shows body_drain is already about 1us/request and the diagnostic still fails SOTA only in HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy while HTTPS-origin scenarios are near parity or faster, so full pre-read storage/copy is not sufficient as a SOTA-scale root cause.

red_green_tests.red_result: failed before implementation because PHASE_RE did not parse response_pre_read_bytes/events and phase fields stayed at 0
red_green_tests.green_result: passed after parser, BenchResult, write_results, and summarize_results were updated

red_green_tests.red_result: failed before implementation with missing add_response_pre_read/response_pre_read_* methods
red_green_tests.green_result: passed after TimeGroup added libcurl_bench-only pre-read counters

source_changes.summary: Adds libcurl_bench-only response_pre_read_bytes and response_pre_read_events counters with accumulation and getters.
Records rem.len() when HTTP/1 response-head decode succeeds while phase timing is enabled.
Aggregates and prints response pre-read byte/event counters in _phase_us output.
Parses, writes, and summarizes response_pre_read_bytes/events phase fields.
Adds regression coverage for response pre-read phase parsing and summary columns.

build_and_test_commands.result: passed with existing stable rustfmt warnings about ignored nightly-only rustfmt options

diagnostic_dataset.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 1 --phase-timing --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_preread_diag_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/response-preread-diagnostic --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/response-preread-diagnostic

confirmed: HTTP/1 response-head read pre-reads the entire 4096-byte response body in every measured ylong request.
ylong body drain sees one 4096-byte chunk/request because the body is already in PreRead.
The diagnostic has zero client errors and preserves local HTTPS proxy/origin fixture semantics.
refuted_or_limited: Full-body pre-read storage/copy alone is not sufficient to explain the remaining SOTA gap: body_drain is about 0.9-1.0us/request, and HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy/proxy-mTLS scenarios are near parity or faster in this single-concurrency diagnostic.
This does not replace the fair concurrency=8 SOTA matrix because phase timing is restricted to concurrency=1 by the benchmark binary.
interpretation.next_hypothesis: If S9 continues, the next candidate should target the lower-level response-read wait/TLS BIO/runtime interaction or a protocol-level flagship scenario rather than PreRead storage alone.

verdict.reason: The diagnostic is causal narrowing evidence, not a SOTA completion run. It confirms full response pre-read but does not produce or justify a SOTA-satisfying implementation; S9 remains active and S10 must not run as a completion gate.
